purpose: Reusable confirmation-dialog primitives for destructive git operations. Exports two components: ConfirmDialog (single-step) and DoubleConfirmDialog (two-step with optional type-to-confirm challenge). Both support three danger levels — danger / warning / safe — each with its own icon (DangerIcon triangle, WarningIcon circle-i, SafeIcon checkmark) and confirm-button class (confirm-danger / confirm-warning / confirm-safe). DoubleConfirmDialog renders ConfirmDialog twice in sequence (step 1 → step 2) with the second step typically requiring the user to type confirmText (e.g. 'DISCARD') exactly to enable the final confirm. Both support an `affectedItems` array that renders as a scrollable list inside the body — useful for showing exact files that will be lost.

exports.name: ConfirmDialog
exports.signature: { isOpen?: boolean, open?: boolean, title, message, dangerLevel?: 'danger'|'warning'|'safe', level?: ..., confirmLabel?, cancelLabel?, confirmText?: string|null, affectedItems?: string[], loading?: boolean, onConfirm, onCancel }

gotchas: Dual prop API for visibility: accepts either `isOpen` or `open` (`isVisible = isOpen ?? open ?? false`) — choose one and stick to it; mixing leads to confusion.
Same dual-prop pattern for `dangerLevel` and `level` — both are accepted, dangerLevel takes precedence.
type-to-confirm: when confirmText is set, the input field auto-focuses and Enter submits IF canConfirm — but only the second step of DoubleConfirmDialog uses this typically.
Escape key listener is attached to document and stops propagation — be aware if a parent component also listens for Escape.
Backdrop click triggers onCancel — content area has stopPropagation to prevent accidental dismissal when clicking inside.

DoubleConfirmDialog resets step to 1 every time isVisible becomes false — but onCancel inside step 1 calls onCancel? AFTER setStep(1). On step 2, cancel goes through handleCancel which resets step then calls onCancel.
The default secondMessage is 'This action cannot be undone. All changes will be permanently lost.' — override via secondMessage when more specific guidance is needed.
BranchPanel and RemotePanel implement their OWN inline ConfirmDialogs and do not use this module — when consolidating, audit those files too.
patterns: Three-tier danger level system with consistent icon + button class mapping — used by QuickActions for all destructive flows.
AffectedItems list rendered as a scrollable code-style list (git-manager-dialog-affected) — keeps long file lists readable.
Two-step double-confirm: step 1 is just acknowledge, step 2 has the type-to-confirm input — the actual onConfirm only fires from step 2.
conventions: Inline SVG icons (DangerIcon, WarningIcon, SafeIcon) at top of file; LEVEL_ICONS map keys 'danger'|'warning'|'safe'.
All dialogs use role='dialog' and aria-modal='true' — accessibility baked in.
Class naming: git-manager-dialog-overlay (backdrop) → git-manager-dialog (content with level class) → header/body/footer subdivision.
